scene.description: Engine 181 is stretching a 200' 1¾" preconnect to the front door of a single-family dwelling. The fog nozzle is flowing 150 GPM at 50 PSI. No elevation and no appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure should you set?

problems.title: Basic 1¾ Preconnect to Front Door
problems.question: Engine 181 is stretching 200 ft of 1¾-inch preconnect to the front door. The fog nozzle is flowing 150 GPM at 50 PSI. Use C = 15.5, no elevation, and no appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds of feet.
problems.instructorExplanation: This variation uses 200 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 150 GPM. Calculate the line friction loss, then add 50 PSI nozzle pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Do not reuse another variation’s answer. The hose length and/or flow may change even though the picture looks similar.
